title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. confusus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. confusus, commonly known as the wild daffodil, is a subspecies of the wild daffodil (Narcissus pseudonarcissus), a perennial plant with bright yellow flowers and a strong, pungent scent. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. confusus include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, with the flowers and leaves being used to create the medicinal preparations.
